title: Lapithea boykinii
taxonomy: Lapithea boykinii is a species of flowering plant in the family Aizoaceae. It is native to South Africa, where it is commonly found growing in sandy areas. The plant is classified as a succulent, characterized by its thick, fleshy leaves that help it store water. Within the Aizoaceae family, Lapithea boykinii is part of the subfamily Ruschioideae. The classification of Lapithea boykinii is further supported by its unique leaf and flower morphology.
